programmes.show_description: (1984) Oscar-winning real-life drama. American reporter Sydney Schanberg and his translator Dith Pran are separated during the Cambodian Civil War, putting Dith Pran in mortal danger.
(2021) Thriller. Nicolas Cage delivers a subtle turn in Michael Sarnoski's debut, an intriguing tale of an off-the-grid truffle hunter who returns to the city to rescue his kidnapped pig.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(1941) American frontier pioneering western starring Randolph Scott, Robert Young and Dean Jagger as three very different men working to connect east and west by telegraph in the 1860s.
(1957) Comedy. Kenneth More stars as the perfect butler, who assumes command when he, his aristocratic employer (Cecil Parker) and his family and friends are shipwrecked on a desert island.
(1965) Thriller. Sidney Poitier plays a photojournalist aboard a destroyer whose commander's obsessive hunt for a Soviet sub could plunge the world into nuclear war.
(1954) Classic 'cowboys and Indians' Western. Gary Cooper, Richard Widmark and Cameron Mitchell star as three rascals hired by Susan Hayward to rescue her husband from Apache territory.
(2016) Action-comedy with Dwayne Johnson and Kevin Hart. Two former school buddies team up to thwart a terrorist plot. But things may not be as they seem. With Amy Ryan and Jason Bateman.
(2019) Fourth instalment of the sci-fi comedy franchise with Chris Hemsworth, Tessa Thompson and Liam Neeson. An alien-obsessed rookie joins London's Men in Black team.
(2019) Keanu Reeves returns for the third time as the supposedly retired hitman. Someone's put a contract on John Wick's head and only a mystic in Morocco can lift it.
(2019) Horror twist on the fairy-tale paradigm with Riley Keough. A future stepmother tries to gain the trust of her partner's children. But the kids will stop at nothing to get rid.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
(2014) Jamie Foxx stars in this reboot of the 1982 musical. Orphan Annie is transported to post-2008 recession-hit New York, where she becomes the ward of a germaphobe running for mayor.
(1962) War drama. Darryl F Zanuck's classic Oscar-winning recreation of the Allied landings in Normandy on 6 June 1944, with an all-star British, American, German and French cast.
Director Michael Pearce and star Jessie Buckley talk about making the dark British drama set on Jersey.
(2020) Comedy-drama with Liam Neeson. An estranged father and son team up to fix up and sell an old Tuscan villa that their late wife and mother left to them.
(2022) Romcom with Julia Roberts and George Clooney. A staggeringly beautiful, divorced couple reunite to stop their daughter's wedding in the staggeringly beautiful surroundings of Bali.
(2000) Fact-based legal drama. Julia Roberts won an Oscar, Golden Globe and BAFTA for her portrayal of a single mum making a huge impact at a legal firm despite having no formal training.
